name: Vegetable Lasagna Roll-Ups
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Fresh spinach, washed and coarsely chopped
1 can (15 oz) white kidney beans, drained and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
Sauce:
3 tsp Extra-light olive oil with a dash of sesame oil
2 cups Chopped onions
2 cloves Garlic, peeled and chopped
2 cups Finely diced eggplant
1 large Red bell pepper, seeded and finely diced
2 tbsp Low-sodium tomato paste
1/4 tsp Red pepper flakes
1 cup Red wine
16 oz Whole tomatoes with liquid
1 tbsp Freshly squeezed lemon juice
1/2 tsp Freshly ground salt
1/2 tsp Freshly ground black pepper
2 tbsp Freshly grated Parmesan cheese
Combine in a large mixing bowl the three cheeses, the spinach, half the beans, half the oregano, and half the basil. Set aside.
Cook the noodles al dente. Cool them under cold water when they're cooked to your liking.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Pour 1 teaspoon of oil into a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry onions and garlic for 5 minutes, stirring often. Transfer half of the spinach and cheese filling to one side and set the other half aside.
Add 1 teaspoon of oil to the same pan and cook eggplant over medium heat for 8 minutes, stirring often. Ensure the bottom of the pan does not scorch. Spoon cooked eggplant into the filling mixture and stir well.
Wipe the pan clean. Add remaining oil to the same pan and cook red bell pepper over medium heat for 3 minutes. Spoon cooked pepper into the filling mixture. Wipe the pan clean.
Add tomato paste to the same pan and cook over medium heat until it turns brown, about 5 minutes. Do not let it burn but just brown. Stir in red pepper flakes and wine, bring to a boil, lower heat to simmer, and reduce liquid by about one-fourth for about 15 minutes. Add canned tomatoes with their liquid, stirring until tomatoes break into pieces.
Add remaining beans, oregano, basil, reserved cooked onions, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes at very low heat. Stir in lemon juice, salt, pepper, and mix well.
Lay a lasagna noodle flat on a cutting board. Form half a cup of the filling into a rough ball, place along one end of the noodle, and roll it up end to end. Repeat with remaining noodles.
Pour sauce into a 9×13 baking pan. Place lasagna rolls seam side down, spoon some sauce over them. Cover with aluminum foil and bake for 40 minutes. Remove foil, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, and bake for 5 more minutes.
